frame_analyses.description: Needle is being loaded onto the needle driver.
Suture is placed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement is close to the mark with minimal deviation.

frame_analyses.description: First throw of the surgeon's knot is being tied.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw observed, proper tension applied.

frame_analyses.description: Switching hands for the second single throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch is apparent, proper tension maintained.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw being performed with an alternate hand switch.
Both ends of the suture are being cut within the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cutting of suture ends observed.

frame_analyses.description: Procedure is complete with both suture ends cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final inspection shows no visible gaps in the slit.

knot_assessments.notes: Surgeon's knot secured with double throw.
Single throw completed securely with hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap, slit is properly closed.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated good needle handling and knot tying with secure and properly tensioned throws. Suture placement showed minimal deviation and the drain slit was fully closed without gaps.
improvement_suggestions: Reduce suture placement deviation for higher accuracy
Practice consistent timing across phases for efficiency
